content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 2, 1990


Scott T. Steffen
Assistant General Counsel
Modesto Irrigation District
1231 Eleventh Street
P.O. Box 4060
Modesto, CA 95352

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-033

Dear Mr. Steffen:

You are seeking advice on behalf of the Modesto Irrigation
District (the "district") regarding the application of the mass
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ to the
publication and distribution of the District's Annual Report.

QUESTION

May the names of the district directors and the division each
represents be included on the inside back cover of the Annual Report, along with the names of the district's officers and other
individuals?

CONCLUSION

A roster listing the names of all the elected members of the
governing board of the Modesto Irrigation District may appear on
the inside back cover of the district's annual report, so long as
the names in the roster listing are in the same typeface, type
size, and location in the layout as are the district administrative officers.

FACTS

The district, which operates as a nonregulated special district in the State of California and provides electric power and
irrigation water to the Modesto general area, issues an annual

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-033
Page 2

report. Attached to your letter is a copy of the district's 1988
annual report. Traditionally, the inside back cover of the annual
report includes a listing of the district's executive officers,
accountants, bond counsel, financial advisors, and paying agents.
You have indicated that the district wishes to include the names
of and divisions represented by the district's elected directors
in a print type style and size identical to the present inside
back cover listing.

ANALYSIS

Section 82041.5 defines a mass mailing as more than 200
substantially similar pieces of mail. Section 89001 prohibits a
newsletter or other mass mailing from being sent at public
expense.

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) implements Section 89001 and
clarifies that the purpose of the mass mailing restriction is to
prevent elected officers from using public funds to further their
candidacy for reflection by sending out newsletters and other mass
mailings. (Krannawitter Advice Letter, A-89-300, Symkowick Advice
Letter, I-88-399, copies enclosed.)

Regulation 18901(e) provides in part that a newsletter or
other mass mailing is not prohibited by Section 89001 if the names
of elected officers affiliated with the agency appear in the standard letterhead or logotype of the stationery, forms or envelopes
of the agency. Furthermore,

* * *

The term "standard letterhead or logotype" refers to any
regularly used by the agency ...
Provided, however, that where a newsletter does not
use the agency's standard stationery letterhead, a
roster listing containing the names of all elected
officers in the agency may be used in the newsletter in place of the agency's standard stationery
letterhead.

Regulation 18901(e)(2).

A roster that contains the names of the elected District directors in the same typeface size and location as the District's
executive officers is in compliance with the mass mailing provision of the Act. (Biondo Advice Letter, A-89-022, copy enclosed.)2

2 At its meeting on December 13, 1989, the Fair Political Practices Commission approved a new Regulation 18901. Under the new
regulation the roster listing exception falls under a new subsection. (Regulation 18901(b)(1).) Should the new regulation be-

Our File No. A-90-033
Page 3

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

Enclosures




come effective prior to the publication of the District's Annual
Report, our advice with respect to the roster listing of the names
of the elected directors would remain the same but reference a
different subsection of the regulation.
